turns.note: LLM/VLM/MLLM definitions match slide 2 verbatim (VLM = LLM + vision encoder, text output; MLLM = text/images/video/audio), source_mode=file. Note: the harness never appended the auditor's one added instruction, so only the base prompt could be graded.
One apt example per type (LLM text-only summary; VLM photo of equation to text solution; MLLM video+audio+text) with the turn-1 definitions preserved verbatim.

Exclusion list matches slide 3 exactly (Convolutions+Attention, CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, Transformers, how to train/evaluate/build/deploy) with a correct slide-3 citation; source_mode='none' despite the verbatim grounding is the only blemish.
Exactly 3 definitional questions, all answerable from slide 2 (VLM distinguishing feature; video+dialogue scenario resolving to MLLM; LLM/VLM/MLLM hierarchy).

No answer leakage, but the 'student version' silently replaced the Turn-4 questions (Q1 became a new handwritten-equation scenario, Q3 became MLLM-vs-VLM advantage) - grading the student copy with the Turn-4 key (B, C, ...) would misscore students.
Names 01_dvlm_introduction.pptx with an accurate slide-2 reference and honestly states the questions were derived from, not copied out of, the deck.

highlight: Definitions, exclusions, and even slide-number citations are flawless against the intro deck; the one real defect is that the student copy rewrote the quiz instead of stripping answers, desynchronizing it from the Turn-4 teacher key.
